title: RBAC Roles and Permissions Semantic Contract
description: Draft semantic contract for role and permission evaluation supporting authentication and request authorization. The first implementation slice covers static permission allow/deny, dynamic policy allow/deny, step-up/authentication-required denial, accessible UI denial, audit-query permission filtering, and tenant-boundary preservation.
aiGuidance: Permissions are semantic enforcement primitives. Do not replace permission checks with role-name resemblance during implementation extraction.

lifecycle.description: The capability assigns roles and deterministically resolves them into permissions: tenant membership roles (tenant-admin, manager, member, viewer) are assigned through the member role-update path, system-admin is a global role that is rejected as a membership role, and any role change re-resolves the actor's permission bundle. Roles are convenience bundles while permissions are the enforcement primitive.

stateModel.description: State is the role vocabulary split into a global role (system-admin) and tenant roles (tenant-admin, manager, member, viewer), with role-to-permission bundles resolved through a fixed permission map and membership status tracked separately. The resolved permission set, not the role name, is what downstream authorization consumes.

permissions.description: Routes enforce explicit required permissions such as tenant.members.*, tenant.groups.*, tenant.suborgs.*, and platform.* and never the role name directly, with platform.admin.access kept distinct from tenant.admin.access. Permission resolution is tenant-scoped so a tenant role only yields that tenant's permissions.

contracts.description: The contract is the role and permission vocabulary published by the identity domain and drift-checked against the admin contract's tenant-role and membership-status definitions, surfaced operationally through the member role and status update operations. The vocabulary is a versioned semantic surface rather than a runtime route set.

validation.description: Membership validation requires user id, organisation id, and a role and rejects system-admin as a tenant role, and permission resolution is proven to produce the correct bundles per role including the split between platform and tenant admin access. Unknown or invalid role values are rejected before any state change.

errorModel.description: Invalid role updates return an invalid-body outcome, missing memberships return not-found, and last-admin demotion, removal, or disable return explicit unprocessable-entity validation outcomes; permission denial is handled fail-closed by the request pipeline before any route handler runs. No partial role state is left on a rejected change.

auditModel.description: Role and status mutations emit audit events, and audit failure aborts the role change before the underlying store is mutated. Pure read-side permission resolution is not itself audited.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is invariant-ready because permission resolution is a pure, fully-tested function and route enforcement is exercised through pipeline permission tests; degraded mode disables store-dependent mutation while keeping read-only resolution available. There is no external provider dependency for resolution itself.

proof.description: Unit proofs cover the role/permission domain, member role mutations, and contract drift between the domain vocabulary and the admin contract, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic role/permission state validation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: UI permission gating renders an accessible forbidden state when the actor lacks the required permission and must not duplicate authentication semantics.
